query: JPM catalysts, trend confirmation, risk factors and fundamental context for this week's investment analysis

matches.title: Asian Investors Repositioning Portfolios, JPMorgan's Craig Says
matches.text: Kerry Craig of JPMorgan Asset Management says Asian markets are experiencing continued repositioning by investors. He sees the moves as driven by rotation within portfolios rather than thinking about "a big flight away from risk assets at the moment."

matches.title: How JPMorgan’s New Target Is Shaping The Evolving Story At Inchcape (LSE:INCH)
matches.text: JPMorgan has lifted its price target for Inchcape to £8.80, giving investors an updated reference point for what the shares might be worth under refreshed assumptions. Bullish and cautious analysts alike are using this new target to frame a debate around how the latest inputs affect perceived risk and reward. As you read on, you will see how to interpret this evolving story and what it could mean for your own view on Inchcape. Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head...

matches.title: JPMorgan: Iran War A 'Modest Macro-Economic Shock'
matches.text: JPMorgan Head of Asia Economics and Chief India Economist Sajjid Chinoy says the conflict in Iran represents a 'modest macro-economic shock', that's unlikely to derail global expansion. He speaks with Haslinda Amin on "Insight with Haslinda Amin."

matches.title: Tracking the gold trade: 3 reasons Wall Street is still bullish
matches.text: Gold (GC=F) prices have been volatile this week due to impacts from the Iran war, but overall, the precious metal has rallied over the past few years as investors continue to seek safety amid increased uncertainty. matches.title: JP Morgan CEO has blunt inflation message
matches.text: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon stepped in front of the cameras Monday with Wall Street desperate for a measured voice.
